title: identity — cache keys + tags
description: Identity caches are AGGRESSIVELY short-TTL. Credentials MUST NOT be cached (safety); lookups by ID / email are cached for < 60 seconds so a password change / lockout / erasure propagates fast. Every cache key is namespaced by tenant scope where applicable — EXCEPT the Identity itself, which is global and cross-tenant.

keys.description: Identity ID resolved from email (via SHA-256 hash of normalised email, NEVER the plaintext). Read by every login attempt. Very short TTL so a soft-delete propagates within one minute.
Cached Identity row (redacted — no password_hash, no mfa_secret_encrypted). Speed lookups from downstream User rows that need the shared credentials shape.

In-flight MFA challenge state (created by identity/auth's MfaChallengeAction). 5-minute TTL and hard-consumed on verify. Stored in Redis (not the DB) so a spike in challenge throughput doesn't hit Postgres.
Per-IP login attempt counter for identity.rate_limit_login middleware. 15-minute sliding window.
invalidatedBy: successful login clears the (ip, email) tuple

keys.description: Per-email login attempt counter. 1-hour sliding window.
24-hour cache of the HIBP response for a specific 5-character SHA-1 prefix. Survives HIBP outages + reduces outbound-call volume by an order of magnitude.
Current KMS-generated data key (encrypted-form + plaintext-form) held in-memory for MFA-secret encryption. Rotated at process start OR after 1000 uses OR every 15 minutes.
keys.notes: This is a MEMORY-ONLY cache — NEVER persisted to Redis. Listed here for documentation only.

keys.description: Denormalised gauge feeding the `academorix.identity.active_lockouts` metric. Refreshed by a scheduled task every 60s.
